sanskrit:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा।
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः॥
transliteration:
maharṣayaḥ sapta pūrve catvāro manavas tathā |
madbhāvā mānasā jātā yeṣāṃ loka imāḥ prajāḥ ||
literal_meaning: The seven great sages, the four earlier ones, the Manus born of My mind—these are the ancestors of all beings in the world.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that every lineage of thought, feeling, and action ultimately originates from a single creative source within consciousness itself.
psychological_state: Expansion of self-concept from isolated individual to member of an immense, meaningful genealogy of mind.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling a family-tree app and realizing you’re connected to thousands of strangers; simultaneously feeling small yet part of something vast.
anchor_text: Every thought and action flows from one conscious source.
